ucs.title: [LLM] APT5 (Mulberry Typhoon / UNC2630) LSASS credential dump via rundll32 + comsvcs.dll MiniDump
ucs.description: Hunts the LOLBIN credential-access tradecraft APT5 reliably uses after pivoting from a compromised Pulse Secure / Citrix NetScaler appliance into the Windows estate — invoking rundll32.exe against C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll with the MiniDump (ordinal #24) export, against the LSASS PID. APT5 prefers LOLBIN credential extraction over dropping a Mimikatz binary, so this combo (rundll32 + comsvcs + MiniDump/#24) is a high-fidelity signature of the cluster Mandiant tracks as UNC2630 / Microsoft tracks as Mulberry Typhoon.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" AND (Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll,*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll *MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll,#24*" OR Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll #24*" OR Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll,MiniDumpW*") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_id Processes.process_hash _time 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
| eval target_pid = case(match(process, "(?i)\sMiniDump\s+(\d+)\s"), 1) 
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) 
| sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// APT5 / Mulberry Typhoon — LSASS dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBIN
let _machine_accounts = dynamic(["system","local service","network service"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs"                       // indexed term
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)comsvcs\.dll[ ,]+(MiniDump(W)?|#24)\b"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ (_machine_accounts)
// Pull the LSASS PID argument when present — third positional arg after MiniDump
| extend TargetPid = extract(@"(?i)comsvcs\.dll[ ,]+(?:MiniDump(?:W)?|#24)\s+(\d+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend DumpPath  = extract(@"(?i)(?:MiniDump(?:W)?|#24)\s+\d+\s+([\"']?[A-Za-z]:\\[^\s\"']+|\\\\\?\\[A-Z]:\\[^\s\"']+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          ProcessCommandLine, TargetPid, DumpPath,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentIntegrity = InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel,
          IsRemote = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT5 / Mulberry Typhoon — LSASS dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBIN (Sentinel — needs 4688 cmd-line auditing)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith @"\rundll32.exe"
| where CommandLine has "comsvcs"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)comsvcs\.dll[ ,]+(MiniDump(W)?|#24)\b"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE")
| extend TargetPid = extract(@"(?i)comsvcs\.dll[ ,]+(?:MiniDump(?:W)?|#24)\s+(\d+)", 1, CommandLine)
| extend DumpPath  = extract(@"(?i)(?:MiniDump(?:W)?|#24)\s+\d+\s+([\"']?[A-Za-z]:\\[^\s\"']+)", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, CommandLine, TargetPid, DumpPath,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, SubjectUserName, SubjectLogonId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT5 LSASS Credential Dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump
id: a5c0d9c1-5c5a-4d3e-9c7b-8f2a3d5b1e44
status: experimental
description: Detects rundll32.exe invoking comsvcs.dll's MiniDump (ordinal #24) export — a LOLBIN credential-dumping technique repeatedly observed from APT5 / UNC2630 / Mulberry Typhoon following Pulse Secure & Citrix NetScaler appliance compromise.
references:
  - https://media.defense.gov/2022/Dec/13/2003131586/-1/-1/0/CSA-APT5-CITRIX-ADC-V1.PDF
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/check-your-pulse-suspected-apt-actors-leverage-authentication-bypass
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1023/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.t1218.011
  - apt5
  - mulberry_typhoon
  - unc2630
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'comsvcs.dll,MiniDump'
      - 'comsvcs.dll MiniDump'
      - 'comsvcs.dll,#24'
      - 'comsvcs.dll #24'
      - 'comsvcs.dll,MiniDumpW'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_img and selection_cmd and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Extremely rare; some legacy backup / crash-dump tooling shells out to comsvcs.dll MiniDump
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT5 / UNC2630 has been called out by NSA (Dec 2022 Citrix ADC advisory) and Mandiant (Pulse Secure zero-day reporting) for using LOLBIN credential dumping rather than dropping known tooling like Mimikatz or ProcDump — comsvcs.dll's MiniDump function (rundll32 ordinal #24) is the canonical instance. The combination of rundll32.exe + comsvcs.dll + MiniDump/#24 + LSASS PID is virtually never legitim
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma tag must start with namespace separated with dot from remaining tag.

ucs.title: [LLM] APT5 staging and RAR-archiving collected data in non-user paths (PerfLogs / ProgramData / Public)
ucs.description: Hunts APT5's documented collection-and-exfil-prep pattern: dropping a renamed rar.exe (commonly r.exe / rar32.exe) into non-user paths such as C:\PerfLogs, C:\ProgramData, C:\Users\Public, then producing password-protected, multi-volume archives of staged loot prior to exfil over webshell / VPN tunnel. The combination of -hp<password>, -v split volumes and a non-user staging directory is signature for this Chinese cluster (Mulberry Typhoon / Keyhole Panda).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("rar.exe","rar32.exe","winrar.exe","r.exe") OR Processes.original_file_name IN ("Rar.exe","WinRAR.exe")) AND Processes.process="* a *" AND (Processes.process="*-hp*" OR Processes.process="*-p*") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
| eval suspicious_path = if(match(mvjoin(cmdlines," "), "(?i)C:\\\\(PerfLogs|ProgramData|Users\\\\Public|Windows\\\\Temp|\$Recycle\.Bin|Intel|Recovery)\\\\"), 1, 0) 
| eval renamed_archiver  = if(process_name!="winrar.exe" AND process_name!="rar.exe", 1, 0) 
| eval password_flag     = if(match(mvjoin(cmdlines," "), "(?i)\s-hp\S"), 1, 0) 
| eval split_volume      = if(match(mvjoin(cmdlines," "), "(?i)\s-v\d+"), 1, 0) 
| where suspicious_path=1 OR renamed_archiver=1 OR (password_flag=1 AND split_volume=1) 
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) 
| sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// APT5 / Mulberry Typhoon — RAR archive staging in non-user paths
let _stage_paths = dynamic([@"C:\PerfLogs\", @"C:\ProgramData\", @"C:\Users\Public\", @"C:\Windows\Temp\", @"C:\Intel\", @"C:\Recovery\", @"C:\$Recycle.Bin\"]);
let _rar_names    = dynamic(["rar.exe","rar32.exe","winrar.exe","r.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where ProcessCommandLine has " a "        // rar add command
// Either the binary is named rar/winrar/r/rar32, OR cmdline carries the rar password+volume signature regardless of name (renamed binary)
| where (FileName in~ (_rar_names))
     or (ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName in~ ("Rar.exe","WinRAR.exe"))
     or (ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-hp\S+.+\s-v\d+")
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-hp[\S]{4,}"   // password flag with non-empty password
// Output is going to (or input from) a known staging directory
| extend StagingHit = iff(ProcessCommandLine has_any (_stage_paths)
                       or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_stage_paths)
                       or FolderPath has_any (_stage_paths), true, false)
| extend RenamedRar = iff(FileName !in~ ("rar.exe","winrar.exe")
                       and ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName in~ ("Rar.exe","WinRAR.exe"), true, false)
| extend SplitArchive = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-v\d+[kmg]?"
| where StagingHit or RenamedRar or SplitArchive
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          BinaryOnDisk = FileName, OriginalName = ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName,
          ProcessCommandLine, FolderPath, SHA256,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          StagingHit, RenamedRar, SplitArchive
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT5 / Mulberry Typhoon — RAR archive staging via 4688 process-create
let _stage_paths = dynamic([@"C:\PerfLogs\", @"C:\ProgramData\", @"C:\Users\Public\", @"C:\Windows\Temp\", @"C:\Intel\", @"C:\Recovery\", @"C:\$Recycle.Bin\"]);
let _rar_names    = dynamic([@"\rar.exe",@"\rar32.exe",@"\winrar.exe",@"\r.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where CommandLine has " a "
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-hp[\S]{4,}"
| extend NameMatch  = NewProcessName has_any (_rar_names)
| extend StagingHit = CommandLine has_any (_stage_paths) or NewProcessName has_any (_stage_paths)
| extend SplitArchive = CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-v\d+[kmg]?"
| where NameMatch or StagingHit or SplitArchive
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, NameMatch, StagingHit, SplitArchive, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT5 RAR-Based Data Staging in Non-User Paths
id: 6b1f2d8e-3a72-4d1d-a3aa-5e2c11f9c7d2
status: experimental
description: Detects renamed/native rar.exe being used to create password-protected (-hp), often multi-volume (-v) archives written to non-user staging paths (PerfLogs, ProgramData, Users\Public, Windows\Temp, Recovery, Recycle.Bin) — the documented collection / exfil-prep pattern of APT5 (Mulberry Typhoon / UNC2630 / Keyhole Panda).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1023/
  - https://media.defense.gov/2022/Dec/13/2003131586/-1/-1/0/CSA-APT5-CITRIX-ADC-V1.PDF
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.collection
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.t1074.001
  - attack.t1036.005
  - apt5
  - mulberry_typhoon
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_archiver:
    - Image|endswith:
        - '\rar.exe'
        - '\rar32.exe'
        - '\winrar.exe'
        - '\r.exe'
    - OriginalFileName:
        - 'Rar.exe'
        - 'WinRAR.exe'
  selection_password:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)\s-hp\S{4,}'
  selection_add_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains: ' a '
  selection_context:
    - CommandLine|contains:
        - 'C:\PerfLogs\'
        - 'C:\ProgramData\'
        - 'C:\Users\Public\'
        - 'C:\Windows\Temp\'
        - 'C:\Intel\'
        - 'C:\Recovery\'
        - 'C:\$Recycle.Bin\'
    - CommandLine|re: '(?i)\s-v\d+[kmg]?'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_archiver and selection_password and selection_add_cmd and selection_context and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - IT-managed backup or evidence-collection workflows using WinRAR; baseline known admin paths to exclude
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT5 / Mulberry Typhoon's collection step has been repeatedly attributed to renamed RAR archivers writing password-protected, split archives into non-user directories (PerfLogs, ProgramData, Public, Recovery) so that the staged loot survives user-profile cleanup and is easy to pull back through the same VPN/Citrix appliance webshell they used to enter (T1505.003). The signature combo — `-hp<pw>` +
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma tag must start with namespace separated with dot from remaining tag.
